name: S. aureus sensor (AIP/AgrC + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects Staphylococcus aureus by sensing its autoinducing peptide (AIP). The AgrC/AgrA quorum receptor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in safe Gram-positive Bacillus subtilis 168 — no live pathogen handled.

input.detects: The agr autoinducing peptide secreted by S. aureus

sensing.sensorMechanism: The AgrC histidine kinase detects AIP and phosphorylates AgrA, which activates the agr P2/P3 promoters.
sensing.signalFlow: AIP -> AgrC/AgrA activates P3 -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence when S. aureus signal is present.

parts.name: AgrC/AgrA two-component system
parts.notes: AIP-responsive sensor kinase + activator; Gram-positive peptide signalling fits B. subtilis.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

output.result: Fluorescence indicates S. aureus quorum signal in the sample.

performance.notes: Senses the pathogen's peptide signal, not the live organism; AgrC/AgrA and CRISPRa are validated separately and integration is a design template (requires porting agr into B. subtilis).

safety.biocontainment: Safe GRAS B. subtilis reads a secreted peptide; no S. aureus culture required.
safety.notes: Indirect, safe detection — a key advantage of signal-molecule sensing.

buildSteps.title: Port agr + design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Reconstitute AgrC/AgrA in B. subtilis; design the CRISPRa sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: AgrC/AgrA. TU2: P3 -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9-omega. TU4: weak promoter -> sfGFP.

buildSteps.title: Transform B. subtilis 168
buildSteps.description: Natural competence; select; validate AIP-dependent activation.

buildSteps.title: Add sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample + synthetic AIP standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the S. aureus AgrC/AIP quorum module with B. subtilis CRISPRa
partsSources: Novick & Geisinger 2008 / agr quorum sensing (S. aureus)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
